title: Willawarrin / Bellbrook Recurring Drinking Water Failure
investigation_objective: Establish the full, source-verified chronology of recurring drinking-water supply failures affecting Willawarrin and Bellbrook (trucked water episodes at least in 2020 and 2023, tied to high iron/manganese levels in the Macleay River during low-flow periods), and track the $8 million Willawarrin Water Treatment Plant intended as the permanent fix through to a confirmed completion or delay - resolving whether trucked water is still in use today.
scope: In scope: council and NSW Government primary sources on water-supply incidents, dam/river extraction decisions, and the treatment plant's funding, construction, and commissioning status for Willawarrin and Bellbrook specifically. Out of scope for this case: general Kempsey Shire-wide water policy not tied to these two communities, and the separate Bellbrook flying-fox, asbestos, streets/roads, Armidale Road, and communications issues, which are tracked as their own matters.
